element_introduction_order.mastery_context: infinite-use baseline weapon, introduces basic combat mechanics
area damage tool, requires tactical timing
gear shifting, vehicle combat, established immediately as core mechanic
genre transition, target reticle control, grenade vs bullets

element_introduction_order.first_appears: Game intro and between every stage
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: plot revelation, character development, contextual stakes building
plot twist, emotional stakes, boss encounter
climactic horror reveal, emotional climax before final stage

typical_relief_triggers: safe platform areas with no enemies
genre transitions (shift to driving/shooting breaks platforming tension)
typical_tension_triggers: moving targets in first-person shooting segments
plot revelations in cutscenes (narrative tension escalation)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Cyclical multi-genre rhythm with narrative punctuation. Each stage alternates between action intensity and cutscene narrative breaks. Driving and shooting stages function as mechanical pacing shifts rather than difficulty valleys. Three distinct mechanical contexts (platforming/driving/shooting) prevent monotonic tension curves and force player re-adaptation every stage or two.

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction stages (Route 246, Waterfront) establish driving and basic platforming mechanics with forgiving enemy patterns. Laser whip teaches safe combat. Driving stage at start sets tone for genre hybrid expectation.
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stages 3-7 (Chinatown, Port, Ricardo Range, Warehouse, Train/Sewers) escalate enemy density and introduce cyborg mini-boss (Reese), narrative stakes intensify. First-person shooting stage (4-2) introduces new mechanical context. Weapon management becomes critical as ammunition scarcity increases.
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stages 8-11 (Power Plant, Bio-Tech Lab, Harbor, BEDA HQ) feature biological horror and clone experiments, highest enemy density, culminating in director boss fight (human + monster form). Final driving stage forces mastery of all mechanics under extreme pressure.
notable_spikes: Stage 6 boss (Reese cyborg reveal) - emotional and mechanical difficulty
Stage 9 (Bio-Tech Laboratory) - horror narrative spike and mutant transformation
Stage 11 (BEDA HQ final stage) - final director clone battle with multiple forms

notable_level_patterns.description: Game opens with driving stage (Route 246) and closes with driving stage before final HQ. Overhead perspective vehicle combat with gear-shifting and enemy vehicle combat. Functions as mechanical palette cleanser and narrative throughline (investigation chase → corporate escape).

Perspective shift to Hart's viewpoint for reconnaissance/elimination missions. Target reticle movement combined with grenade/bullet weapon choice. Breaks platforming monotony and signals narrative investigation progression.
Standard side-scrolling parallax stages with left-to-right progression. Enemy encounters clustered at key chokepoints. Weapon switching required. Climbing sections provide vertical relief.

Stages where plot revelation dramatically intensifies emotional and existential stakes. Bio-Tech Laboratory (Stage 9) features human experimentation visuals and Chris transformation, creating narrative climax before mechanical final confrontation.
Train cars and sewer systems offer tight, ladder-heavy platforming. Crouch mechanics become essential. Constrains player movement and creates siege-like pacing against enemies in enclosed spaces.
